data.title: Exxon names low-carbon unit head Ammann as new Upstream president
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) said Tuesday that Liam Mallon, the head of its E&P business, will retire effective February 1 after 34 years with the company, to be succeeded by Dan Ammann.

Mallon has been president of Exxon Mobil Upstream (XOM) since 2022 after holding several leadership roles in Africa, Australia, Asia, and the Americas.

Ammann joined Exxon in 2022 after many years at General Motors to lead the oil company's fledgling Low Carbon Solutions business.

Exxon (XOM) said another former GM executive, Barry Engle, will succeed Ammann as president of the low-carbon business.

Separately, Exxon (XOM) said it is continuing to negotiate with Guyana's government on the terms for developing a shallow-water oil block.

The Exxon-led (XOM) partnership including Hess and Cnooc won offshore block S8 in a bidding round launched in 2022.

data.title: Top 10 SA quant rated Chinese stocks as Trump's tariff strategy targets Beijing
data.description:
President-elect Trump’s latest tariff strategy looks to intensify pressure on China by targeting critical industries which in turn will heighten tensions in the ongoing trade conflict between the world’s two largest economies. This decision has shaken Chinese markets, leaving many investors anxious about the possible economic consequences.

As a result, SA outlined a top 10 list of Chinese stocks with a market capitalization of at least $1B. The list is ranked by SA quant metrics.

Top 10 Chinese Stocks per SA Quant Metrics

No. 10: Xpeng (XPEV), quant rating of 4.54.

No. 9: Yum China Holdings (YUMC), quant rating of 4.67.

No. 8: Bilibili Inc. (BILI), quant rating of 4.68.

No. 7: JD.com (JD), quant rating of 4.85.

No. 6: Atour Lifestyle Holdings Limited (ATAT), quant rating of 4.85.

No. 5: UP Fintech Holding Limited (TIGR), quant rating of 4.86.

No. 4: FinVolution Group (FINV), quant rating of 4.89.

No. 3: Xiaomi Corporation (OTCPK:XIACY), quant rating of 4.91.

Other popular Chinese based stocks that didn’t crack the top 10 were shares of Tencent Holdings (OTCPK:TCEHY), KE Holdings (BEKE), and Alibaba (BABA). SA quant ratings for TCEHY sit at 4.47, BEKE are at 4.42, and BABA are at 3.40.

Moreover, for investors looking to keep a closer eye on Chinese stocks and investments, they can turn towards exchange traded funds as well for a diversified approach to the space. See some popular listed funds below:

Chinese ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), and (KURE).

data.title: Amazon seeks to work with news outlets to improve new Alexa assistant with gen AI - report
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is reaching out to news publishers for licensing their content, which would be used for the next generation of Alexa voice assistant, slated to debut next year with generative AI, Axios reported Tuesday.

The tech giant is preliminarily testing with several publishers who can serve as launch partners for the revamped Alexa late next year, the report said, citing a person familiar with the plans. It is looking to partner with an array of content providers at local and national levels.

The company is currently testing new ways to incorporate news answers into Alexa via text and photo query results but isn't testing generative AI video outputs as part of its revamped Alexa offering at this time, the report said.

Publishers will be compensated for their content via a licensing agreement, and answers provided by Alexa to news queries will be attributed to the publishing partners, according to the report.

The company wants to add more news partners as it scales the devices and platforms Alexa powers going forward, the report said.

data.title: Chips mixed as investors digest Intel CEO departure, await Marvell earnings
data.description:
Semiconductor stocks were mostly mixed on Tuesday as sector investors digested the latest news out of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) and awaited quarterly results from Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL).

Intel shares fell 5% as Wall Street firms and investors debated what the departure of Pat Gelsinger means for the Santa Clara, Calif.-based company.

"This is going to be a tough job for anyone to step into (it was a tough situation when Pat showed up, and things look much worse now)," said Bernstein Societe Generale Group analysts, led by Stacy Rasgon, in a note. "The choice for any new CEO would seem to center on what to do with the fabs. But while keeping them feels like deadweight, scrapping them would also be fraught with difficulties around the product roadmap, outsourcing strategy, CHIPS act and political navigation."

On Monday, some analysts and investors suggested that Intel's board of directors needs to make a "radical change" with its next CEO.

Former Intel board member and CEO of Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) Lip-Bu Tan has been mentioned as a possible replacement.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) were little changed on Tuesday, even as Amazon (AMZN) announced at its AWS re:Invent cloud computing conference that its new Trainium3 chips would be available next year and its Trainium2 processors are now "generally available."

Nvidia dominates the artificial intelligence accelerator space, with some estimates suggesting it has 95% of the market, with the rest taken by AMD. Amazon shares were fractionally higher.

Separately on Tuesday, four Chinese semiconductor industry associations said processors made in the U.S. are "no longer safe" in response to the newly enacted export control curbs by the Biden administration.

Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) shares skidded roughly 7% in London as South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol unexpectedly announced martial law and put the country under military control.

A few hours after the surprise announcement, the South Korean parliament voted unanimously to rescind the order and lift the martial law declaration.

Marvell shares rose fractionally as investors awaited its third-quarter results. A consensus of analysts are expecting the company to earn $0.41 per share on $1.46B in revenue. Competitor Broadcom (AVGO) was also fractionally higher in midday trading.

Research firm Wells Fargo weighed in on the analog and industrial portions of the market and said some hedge funds are going long On Semiconductor (ON) and short NXP Semiconductors (NXPI). Shares of both companies fell roughly 2% in midday trading.

Several S&P 500 semiconductors saw modest moves lower, including Qualcomm (QCOM), Analog Devices (ADI) and Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR). Micron Technology (MU) bucked the trend and tacked on more than 2%.

data.title: South Korean parliament votes to block president's martial law declaration (update)
data.description:
South Korean President, Yoon Suk Yeol declared a state of emergency and martial law in a sudden late night address to the country, which was soon met with opposition from lawmakers who voted early on Wednesday to block the move. The speaker of parliament declared the martial law announcement invalid.

South Korea’s won slowly started paring back some losses after falling dramatically against the U.S. dollar on Tuesday, after the president's announcement.

The U.S. Dollar (USD:KRW) was last seen trading up more than 1.3% against the South Korean currency, which touched a two-year low on the martial law declaration.

In an unannounced address broadcast live on the country’s YTN television, Yoon attacked his domestic political opponents, and claimed that we would eradicate "shameless pro-North Korean anti-state forces".

Yoon did not mention any specific danger from the neighboring North Korea, which has lately been a nuclear threat to the country.

Other South Korean assets also tumbled on the news, as investors grew concerned about political turmoil and unrest in the country. The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) was last down 2.84% after falling more than 6% in US trading, its worst intraday fall since August.

Several other ETFs also slumped on the announcement, such as the Franklin FTSE South Korea ETF (FLKR) which was down 2.28%, after dropping more than 5.6%, while Direxion Daily South Korea Bull 3X Shares ETF (KORU) was down 8%, after falling more than 20% after the declaration.

U.S. listed shares of Korea-based companies also reacted sharply. Shares of e-commerce company Coupang (CPNG) was down more than 4%, steel processor Posco Holdings (PKX) fell as much as 6.5%, while shares of KB Financial Group (KB) were down 2.62%.

"I declare martial law to protect the free Republic of Korea from the threat of North Korean communist forces, to eradicate the despicable pro-North Korean anti-state forces that are plundering the freedom and happiness of our people, and to protect the free constitutional order," Yoon said.

Yoon’s surprise address shocked the country. He said he had no choice but to resort to such a measure to safeguard constitutional order, claiming that the opposition parties have taken hostage of the parliamentary process.

South Korea's parliament, with 190 of its 300 members present, passed a motion early on Wednesday, after the late night address, requiring the martial law declared by President Yoon Suk Yeol to be lifted.

This story has been updated to add the South Korean parliament's vote to block the president's martial law declaration.

data.title: ADM, Bunge slide as ag giant Cargill to lay off 8,000 workers
data.description:
Archer Daniels Midland (NYSE:ADM) -1.6% and Bunge (NYSE:BG) -1.2% in Tuesday's trading as agriculture peer Cargill said it is laying off 5% of its global workforce, or ~8K workers, as the privately-held commodities giant struggles with weak crop prices following record-breaking results during the pandemic.

Minneapolis-based Cargill has more than 160K employees operating in 70 countries.

Cargill and its crop-trading rivals have suffered from shrinking earnings after bumper crops led to slumping corn and soybean prices, a squeeze that has been magnified at Cargill by the smallest U.S. cattle herd in 70 years, after the company has spent much of the past decade turning itself into the third-largest U.S. beef processor.

Grain traders also face potential trade turbulence under the incoming Trump administration; during president-elect Trump's previous term, U.S. tariffs targeting imports from China and other countries drew retaliatory duties on U.S. crops like soybeans and other farm goods.

Cargill's profits fell 36% to $2.5B in the most recent fiscal year, according to Bloomberg, following record-setting earnings of $6.7B two years ago as the company capitalized on high commodity prices.

data.title: Musk likely to prevail as multi-billion dollar pay package heads to SCOTUS - analysts
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares remain under pressure as the decision handed down by a Delaware Chancery judge will remain a “frustrating headache” for both Musk and shareholders.

“Musk is Tesla and Tesla is Musk,” says Wedbush’s Dan Ives. “One way or another, the board is getting Musk his pay package and another long term one for the next decade,” even if the company must bring this case all the way to the U.S. Supreme Court.

While the latest decision will fuel a protracted battle between Tesla (TSLA) and the courts, Ives believes Tesla (TSLA) will ultimately win this fight at the Supreme Court level given that shareholders have already approved the pay package TWICE in an overwhelming manner.

The appeal will remain an overhang for Tesla (TSLA), especially if the package must be reissued, “but we assume [Musk] is willing to be patient with the process and committed to Tesla,” Barclays Dan Levy says.

The process began in 2018 when Tesla’s (TSLA) board awarded CEO Musk 304M stock options contingent on the company meeting specific operational and financial goals. As the goals were achieved, Musk’s payday seemed assured...and continued to grow, until one of the company’s shareholders sued the company in Delaware court, on the grounds that the package was the result of “sham negotiations” between a beholden board and an intimidating CEO.

Judge Kathaleen McCormick ruled in favor of the shareholder and voided Musk’s compensation plan, prompting the company to redomicile its headquarters from Delaware to Texas. Tesla (TSLA) lawyers assured the Delaware Chancery Court that it retained jurisdiction over Musk’s compensation, and brought the matter before shareholders, citing section 204 of Delaware General Corporation Law which essentially allows companies incorporated in the state the ability to fix “defective corporate acts” without the help of the Chancery court.

When taken to a vote, more than three-quarters of Tesla (TSLA) voting shares approved the pay package, excluding those shares held by Musk and his brother, Kimbal Musk. Tesla (TSLA) attorneys went back to Judge McCormick to reverse her decision based on overwhelming shareholder support.

In her opinion on Monday, McCormick wrote that “even if a stockholder vote could have a ratifying effect, it could not do so here,” maintaining her previous decision while also awarding the plaintiffs $345M in legal fees.

data.title: Vale sees modest gains in iron ore production, surging copper output in coming years
data.description:
Vale (NYSE:VALE) -0.7% in Tuesday's trading after saying at its Investor Day in New York that it expects to produce 325M-335M metric tons of iron ore in 2025, roughly flat compared to output of ~328M tons this year.

The company also said it expects iron ore production will increase modestly in the coming years, forecasting output of 340M-360M metric tons in 2026 and ~360M tons by 2030.

For copper, Vale (VALE) sees production of 345K tons this year, rising to 340K-370K tons in 2025, 350K-380K tons in 2026, 420K-500K tons by 2030, and ~700K tons by 2035.

For nickel, the company guided for output of 160K tons this year, rising to 160K-175K tons in 2025, 175K-210K tons in 2026, and 210K-250K tons by 2030.

Vale (VALE) also said it expects to close the year with a C1 cash cost - direct production costs - of $22/ton, before dropping to $20.50-$22/ton in 2025 and below $20/ton by 2026.

The company also said its sees its capital spending totaling $6.1B this year and ~$6.5B in 2025 and beyond.

data.title: WisdomTree becomes fourth firm to file for spot XRP ETF
data.description:
WisdomTree (NYSE:WT) filed a registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission to seek approval for an ETF that holds digital token XRP (XRP-USD), making it the fourth asset manager to do so.

Since Nov. 26, XRP (XRP-USD), what some also call Ripple, has surged 95% to end Monday at $2.21. On Tuesday, the token fell 8.2% over the past 24 hours to ~$2.52 at 11:45 AM ET, according to CryptoCompare.

WisdomTree joins Bitwise, Canary Capital, and 21Shares in seeking approval for spot XRP ETFs. Earlier this year, the SEC approved ETFs for bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ethereum (ETH-USD). In August 2023, Grayscale Investments won a federal court ruling that ordered the SEC to vacate its rejection of Grayscale's move to convert its bitcoin trust into an ETF.

The WisdomTree filing, made on Monday, didn't yet include the expected sponsor fee. Though it did say that the sponsor fee would be waived for a six-month period for the first $1.0B of the trust's assets. None of the other XRP ETF filings included an expected sponsor fee rate.

Coinbase Global (COIN) subsidiaries will serve as the prime execution agent and custodian for the ETF. Bank of New York Mellon (BK) will be the trust administrator.

data.title: European stocks will see downtrend mid-year 2025 before recovery
data.description:
BofA Securities analysts see a “moderate slowdown” in European equities by the middle of next year, before seeing a recovery by the end of 2025.

Analysts see a 7% downside for the Stoxx 600 (STOXX) to 470 by mid-year, followed by a recovery to 500 by year-end. The index is currently at 515, up 7.7% year-to-date, but down 0.76% from six months ago.

“Our base case of slowing global growth, widening risk premia and lower bond yields is consistent with 7% further underperformance for European cyclicals versus defensives, around 10% renewed underperformance for European value versus growth stocks, as well as 7% outperformance European quality stocks relative to the market,” wrote Thomas Thornton, head of Global Research Product Marketing, in a note.

Here are the top quant-rated European stocks by Seeking Alpha metrics:
